hero.title: Your guide to a smooth and comfortable visit
hero.subtitle: Everything you need to know before your appointment, including what to bring and how scheduling works.

visitBlocks.title: Before Your Appointment
visitBlocks.description: A little preparation helps your visit stay efficient and stress-free.
bullets: Complete paperwork before arrival when possible
Call ahead with questions about fasting, clothing, or prior images

visitBlocks.title: What To Bring
visitBlocks.description: Bring the essentials needed for check-in and accurate care coordination.
bullets: Doctor referral or imaging order if using insurance
Previous reports or images when comparison is helpful

visitBlocks.title: Scheduling and Cost
visitBlocks.description: We aim to keep scheduling simple and pricing easy to understand.
bullets: Call the office to schedule your appointment
Insurance eligibility can be reviewed before the visit
Self-pay pricing is available for straightforward direct-pay options

faqs.question: Do I need a doctor's referral?
faqs.answer: If you are using insurance, a valid referral or order is typically required. For some self-pay visits, your team can guide you on next steps.

faqs.question: How do I schedule an appointment?
faqs.answer: Call the office directly or use the contact page to request a callback. The staff will help match your exam to an available slot.

faqs.question: What insurance plans do you accept?
faqs.answer: Many major plans are accepted. Contact the office for a benefit check before your appointment.

faqs.question: What if I do not have insurance?
faqs.answer: Transparent self-pay rates are available. Reach out for an exact quote based on the study you need.

faqs.question: Do you perform contrast studies?
faqs.answer: If you offer or do not offer contrast studies, update this answer directly in JSON to match your real workflow.

patients.bottomCtaTitle: Have a question before your visit?
patients.bottomCtaText: Our team can help you prepare, verify information, and choose the best next step.

hero.title: Over two decades of patient-focused imaging care
hero.subtitle: A local imaging practice built around clarity, comfort, and dependable service.

about.story: DivineImagesATL Health Imaging was built around a simple idea: diagnostic care should feel organized, compassionate, and transparent. We serve patients, providers, and legal partners with a consistent focus on image quality, communication, and a calm visit experience.

values.title: Patient-centered care
values.description: Comfort, clarity, and respectful treatment shape every part of the visit.

values.title: Clinical excellence
values.description: Modern equipment and dependable processes support accurate diagnostic work.

values.title: Integrity and trust
values.description: Clear communication and straightforward pricing help patients plan with confidence.

values.title: Community focus
values.description: We are committed to serving local families, providers, and referral partners.

hero.title: Advanced MRI scans with clarity and comfort
hero.subtitle: Detailed imaging without radiation, delivered in a calm and professional setting.

mri.intro: MRI uses magnetic fields and radio waves to create highly detailed images of organs, joints, and soft tissues. It is especially useful when clinicians need better visibility into areas that are not captured as clearly on basic imaging.

benefits.title: Non-invasive imaging
benefits.description: MRI does not use ionizing radiation and can support a broad range of diagnostic needs.

benefits.title: Detailed soft tissue evaluation
benefits.description: It provides high-detail visibility for joints, neurological structures, and internal organs.

benefits.title: Better treatment planning
benefits.description: Accurate imaging helps providers make clearer, more confident decisions.

areas.title: Brain and neurological imaging
areas.description: Used when clinicians need high-detail evaluation of the brain and related structures.

areas.title: Spine and musculoskeletal imaging
areas.description: Supports assessment of back pain, disc issues, joint injuries, and other orthopedic needs.

areas.title: Abdominal and pelvic imaging
areas.description: Provides detailed imaging for internal organs and a variety of diagnostic concerns.

faqs.question: What should I expect during my MRI?
faqs.answer: You will be guided through check-in, positioning, and scan preparation. The staff will explain the process and help keep you comfortable.

faqs.question: How should I prepare?
faqs.answer: Wear comfortable clothing and follow any instructions related to metal objects, paperwork, or exam-specific prep.

faqs.question: How long does an MRI take?
faqs.answer: It depends on the study, but many exams are completed within roughly 30 to 60 minutes.

faqs.question: When will my doctor receive results?
faqs.answer: Turnaround depends on the workflow you choose, but results are generally sent to the referring provider as quickly as possible.

hero.title: Get in touch
hero.subtitle: Questions about scheduling, services, insurance, or pricing? Reach out and our team will respond.

contactPage.intro: Use the form for appointment questions, service inquiries, or general support.
contactPage.formSubtitle: This is the only interactive form in the site. Submitted messages are stored in a JSON file.
